steps.title: Read the geometric constraint from the figure
steps.content:
When transversal \(s\) cuts parallel lines \(q\) and \(r\), angles \(x\) and \(y\) are formed at each intersection. From the figure, \(x\) and \(y\) are positioned so that they are co-interior (same-side interior) angles relative to a 155° reference, giving the constraint:
$$x + y = 155$$

steps.title: Set up the system
steps.content:
We have two equations:
  • Algebraic (given): \(y = 2x + 8\)
  • Geometric (figure): \(x + y = 155\)
Substitute \(y = 2x + 8\) into \(x + y = 155\):
$$x + (2x + 8) = 155 \implies 3x = 147 \implies x = 49$$

steps.title: Test all four choices against both constraints
steps.content:
• Choice A — x = 33: \(y = 2(33)+8 = 74\). Check sum: \(33 + 74 = 107 eq 155\). ✗
  • Choice B — x = 41: \(y = 2(41)+8 = 90\). Check sum: \(41 + 90 = 131 eq 155\). ✗
  • Choice C — x = 49: \(y = 2(49)+8 = 106\). Check sum: \(49 + 106 = 155\). ✓
  • Choice D — x = 59: \(y = 2(59)+8 = 126\). Check sum: \(59 + 126 = 185 eq 155\). ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Only x = 49 satisfies both the algebraic equation and the geometric constraint from the figure. That matches choice C — C is correct.
